title: quercus gambelii var. vreelandii
intro: Quercus gambelii var. vreelandii, also known as Vreeland's Gambel oak, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Gambel oak tree native to the southwestern United States. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Vreeland's Gambel oak include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Quercus gambelii var. vreelandii can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and are often used to support overall well-being and promote relaxation.
